---
title: "Semantic Web & Metadata"
subtitle: "Resources and further reading"
---

## Semantic Web Fundamentals

### Recommended Readings

- **W3C RDF Primer** — the official introduction to the Resource Description Framework: [w3.org/TR/rdf11-primer](https://www.w3.org/TR/rdf11-primer/)
- **Linked Data Patterns** — a pattern catalogue for modelling, publishing, and consuming Linked Data: [patterns.dataincubator.org](http://patterns.dataincubator.org/book/)
- **Programming the Semantic Web** (Segaran et al.) — practical introduction with code examples

---

## Dublin Core

- Official specification: [dublincore.org/specifications/dublin-core/](https://dublincore.org/specifications/dublin-core/)
- DCMI Metadata Terms: [purl.org/dc/terms/](http://purl.org/dc/terms/)
- Dublin Core in JSON-LD context: [dublincore.org/specifications/dublin-core/dcmi-terms/](https://www.dublincore.org/specifications/dublin-core/dcmi-terms/)

---

## CIDOC-CRM

- Official site: [cidoc-crm.org](https://cidoc-crm.org)
- Current specification (v7.1.3): [cidoc-crm.org/sites/default/files/cidoc_crm_version_7.1.3.pdf](https://cidoc-crm.org/sites/default/files/cidoc_crm_version_7.1.3.pdf)
- Linked Art (CIDOC-CRM profile for art): [linked.art](https://linked.art)
- CIDOC-CRM Tutorial by George Bruseker: available on the CIDOC-CRM website

---

## Controlled Vocabularies

### GeoNames
- Search interface: [geonames.org/search.html](https://www.geonames.org/search.html)
- API documentation: [geonames.org/export/web-services.html](https://www.geonames.org/export/web-services.html)

### Pactols
- Thesaurus browser: [pactols.frantiq.fr](https://pactols.frantiq.fr)
- Frantiq network: [frantiq.fr](https://frantiq.fr)

### Getty Vocabularies

Published as **Linked Open Data** by the [Getty Research Institute](https://www.getty.edu/research/), the Getty Vocabularies are the standard reference for art history, architecture, and cultural heritage documentation. All terms have stable URIs under `http://vocab.getty.edu/`.

#### AAT — Art & Architecture Thesaurus

~50,000 terms covering object types, styles, materials, techniques, and concepts used in art and architectural description.

```
http://vocab.getty.edu/aat/300263552  →  oil paintings
http://vocab.getty.edu/aat/300014109  →  watercolors
http://vocab.getty.edu/aat/300015012  →  manuscripts
```

🔗 [vocab.getty.edu/aat/](https://vocab.getty.edu/aat/)

#### TGN — Thesaurus of Geographic Names

Over 2 million place entries — historical and current, from cities to archaeological sites. Unlike GeoNames (which focuses on current places), TGN is especially strong on **historical toponyms** and ancient sites.

```
http://vocab.getty.edu/tgn/7011179  →  Salamanca, Spain
http://vocab.getty.edu/tgn/7008038  →  Paris, France
http://vocab.getty.edu/tgn/7001386  →  Constantinople / Istanbul
```

🔗 [vocab.getty.edu/tgn/](https://vocab.getty.edu/tgn/)

#### ULAN — Union List of Artist Names

Biographical and bibliographic information on artists, architects, decorators, and other makers — with variant name forms across languages and periods.

```
http://vocab.getty.edu/ulan/500010570  →  Francisco de Goya
http://vocab.getty.edu/ulan/500115588  →  El Greco
http://vocab.getty.edu/ulan/500021099  →  Rembrandt van Rijn
```

🔗 [vocab.getty.edu/ulan/](https://vocab.getty.edu/ulan/)

#### Linked Data access

All Getty Vocabularies are queryable via SPARQL and available in multiple RDF serialisations:

- SPARQL endpoint: [vocab.getty.edu/sparql](https://vocab.getty.edu/sparql)
- Search interface: [vocab.getty.edu](https://vocab.getty.edu)
- Documentation: [getty.edu/research/tools/vocabularies/lod/](https://www.getty.edu/research/tools/vocabularies/lod/)

::: {.callout-tip}
**In Tropy:** import the Getty AAT vocabulary (`http://vocab.getty.edu/aat.nt`) into Preferences → Vocabularies to use AAT properties in your templates. Use ULAN URIs in `dc:creator` or `dcterms:creator` fields, and TGN URIs in `dcterms:spatial` (alongside or instead of GeoNames).
:::

### Loterre — Linked Open TERminology REsources

Published by [**Inist-CNRS**](https://www.inist.fr), Loterre is a multidisciplinary platform hosting 70+ scientific terminologies as SKOS/RDF Linked Open Data — fully FAIR-compliant and freely accessible.

Unlike Openthéso (a tool for *managing* thesauri) or Pactols (a single domain-specific thesaurus), Loterre is a **portal that aggregates terminologies** from many disciplines and institutions, with a particular emphasis on the French research ecosystem.

**Vocabularies relevant for SSH and CHORAL research:**

| Vocabulary | Scope |
|---|---|
| *Art et Archéologie* | Visual arts, monuments, archaeological objects |
| *Ethnologie* | Peoples, cultures, practices |
| *Histoire et sciences des religions* | Religious history and practices |
| *Linguistique* | Linguistic concepts and terms |
| *Littérature* | Literary forms, genres, movements |
| *Géographie de l'Amérique du Nord* | North American place names |
| *Pays et subdivisions* | Countries and administrative divisions |

**Access:**

- Browse & search: [loterre.fr](https://www.loterre.fr)
- SPARQL endpoint and REST API available for all vocabularies
- Download in SKOS/RDF-XML, Turtle, JSON-LD, or CSV
- Recently integrated into the **ISTEX** infrastructure for text and data mining

::: {.callout-tip}
Loterre also offers **tooling services** for vocabulary producers: validate a SKOS file, transform from other formats, or align your vocabulary with an existing Loterre resource. Useful if you want to publish your own controlled vocabulary as Linked Open Data.
:::

🔗 [loterre.fr](https://www.loterre.fr) · [loterre.istex.fr](https://loterre.istex.fr/fr/)

### Huma-Num Openthéso
- Platform: [opentheso.huma-num.fr](https://opentheso.huma-num.fr)
- GitHub (open-source): [github.com/MOM-CNRS/Opentheso](https://github.com/MOM-CNRS/Opentheso)
- SKOS documentation (W3C): [w3.org/TR/skos-reference/](https://www.w3.org/TR/skos-reference/)

---

## JSON-LD

- Official spec: [json-ld.org](https://json-ld.org)
- JSON-LD Playground (test your JSON-LD): [json-ld.org/playground/](https://json-ld.org/playground/)
